senses.definition: to ignore a situation, topic, issue, etc. so as not to cause troubles or difficulties

examples.x: How about letting sleeping dogs lie huh?
I thought about bringing up my concerns but decided instead to let sleeping dogs lie.

word_list.word: [run] with the hare and [hunt] with the hounds
word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈʌn wɪððə hˈɛɹ ænd hˈʌnt wɪððə hˈaʊndz

senses.definition: to simultaneously support two opposing parties, particularly to increase one's gain

examples.x: Come on, you can't run with the hare and hunt with the hounds—pick a side!
In our office politics, Sally always tries to run with the hare and hunt with the hounds, telling both the clerical workers and the management that she thinks they should prevail.

senses.definition: to take action in order to prevent a probable failure or defeat

examples.x: Edward Heath…was supposed to save the day.
Facing elimination from the playoffs, the team's star quarterback threw an incredible pass for a last-minute touchdown and saved the situation.
The team seemed to be heading for disaster until a late goal saved the day.
The paramedics arrived just in time to save the day and provide life-saving assistance to the injured hiker.
Her negotiation skills saved the situation, and the two parties reached a beneficial agreement.

word_list.word: there is a method to {one's} madness
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz ɐ mˈɛθəd tə wˈʌnz mˈædnəs

senses.definition: used when there is good reasoning behind one's seemingly strange or crazy behavior

examples.x: I know you don't understand my motivation for this decision, but after the dust settles you'll see that there is a method to my madness.
Now that he's started pushing these wild ideas, I sure hope there's a method to his madness—otherwise, he'll be a laughingstock.
Her artistic process seems disorderly, but there's a method to her madness that results in compelling and unique artworks.

word_list.pronunciation: tɹˈiːt hˈændəl ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ wɪð kˈɪd ɡlˈʌvz

senses.definition: to be specially careful, gentle, or considerate when dealing with someone or something

examples.x: She handles her young daughter with kid gloves to avoid upsetting her.
The manager treated the new employee with kid gloves during their first week to help them adjust to the workplace.
In negotiations, it's crucial to handle the sensitive topic with kid gloves to maintain a positive atmosphere.

senses.definition: to make good use of the things that are at one's disposal so as to succeed or gain something

examples.x: If you play your cards right, you could make quite a lot of money out of this.
Soon, if she played her cards right, she would be head of the London office.
what Jamie just did back there cannot be called luck, it's called playing your cards right.
Sarah knew that if she played her cards right in the negotiation, she could secure a better deal.
The CEO played his cards right by investing in new technology, which boosted the company's profits.

senses.definition: to be wise enough to avoid certain behaviors or thoughts

examples.x: He knows better than to mess with a good customer.
She knew better than to go sailing in stormy weather.
Even though he was tempted to skip studying for the test, he knew better and chose to prepare diligently.
I used to believe in get-rich-quick schemes, but now I know better and focus on long-term financial planning.
The company's executives should have known better than to ignore customer complaints for so long.

word_list.word: [make] the best of a bad bargain
word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk ðə bˈɛst əvə bˈæd bˈɑːɹɡɪn

senses.definition: to try to achieve the best outcome possible when dealing with a difficult or bad situation

examples.x: I know this data entry job isn't what you wanted for a career, but for the time being, try and make the best of a bad bargain.
After the car broke down during the road trip, they had to make the best of a bad bargain by exploring the small town while waiting for repairs.
Although the job wasn't what he had hoped for, he decided to make the best of a bad bargain and gain as much experience as possible.
The restaurant served mediocre food, but they made the best of a bad bargain by enjoying each other's company.

senses.definition: to choose a cautious course of action in a situation where there is uncertainty or potential risk

examples.x: I made a few mistakes in my essay, but at least I managed to err on the right side.
When in doubt, it's better to err on the right side of safety and wear your seatbelt.
The manager chose to err on the right side by granting the employee a day off to take care of a family emergency.
In a complex ethical dilemma, it's best to err on the right side and avoid any potential harm.
The judge decided to err on the right side of the law, providing a fair trial for the accused.

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt bˈɪd ðə dˈɛvəl ɡˈʊd mˈɔːɹoʊ ʌntˈɪl tˈɪl ˌɛsbˈiː ˈæktʃuːəli mˈiːt wɪð hˌɪm

senses.definition: used to tell someone to avoid trouble as much as possible

examples.x: Just don't bid the devil good morrow till you meet him.

senses.definition: to follow the accepted or correct path toward reaching one's goal or desired outcome

examples.x: If you went through channels, then they have no reason to turn you away.
You need to go through channels to get the job that is opening up at our company.
